screens.title: What is a prefix?
paragraphs: Prefixes are a group of letters that change the meaning of a word when they are added to the start.
Most prefixes mean a similar thing when they’re added to different words.

screens.title: Today’s prefix: un-
paragraphs: un- is always spelled u-n. It goes at the start of a base word.
un- can mean NOT, or it can REVERSE an action. The base word keeps every letter.

screens.title: Words we will be reviewing today
paragraphs: Look for un- at the start and notice what it does to each base word.

unfair.sentence: It was unfair to change the rules.

unkind.sentence: It was unkind to leave her out.

unlock.sentence: Please unlock the door before we leave.

reflection.promptText: What did you notice about what un- does in these words?

beats.say: First, let’s learn what a prefix is.
beats.narration: Prefixes are a group of letters that change the meaning of a word when they are added to the start. Most prefixes mean a similar thing when they’re added to different words.
un- is always spelled u-n. It goes at the start of a base word. un- can mean not, or it can reverse an action. The base word keeps every letter.

beats.say: These are the words we will explore today.
beats.narration: Today we will review unfair, unkind, unlock, and untidy. Look for un- at the start and notice what it does to each base word.

beats.say: This piece can change the word.
beats.onExplore: Move un- towards happy and notice how the meaning changes.

beats.say: Try the same piece with kind.
beats.onExplore: See whether un- changes kind in the same way.

beats.say: What happens when it meets lock?
beats.onExplore: This time un- reverses an action.
beats.onHelpRequest: Join un- before lock and listen to the new meaning.

beats.say: What happens when it meets tidy?
beats.onExplore: un- makes tidy mean not tidy.
beats.onHelpRequest: Add un- and choose the new meaning.

beats.say: Find the prefix at the start of unhappy.
beats.onSlip: Not there yet. Look for the prefix un- at the front.
beats.onRepeatedMisconception: un- is the first two letters. Chop after the n.
beats.onHelpRequest: un- is the prefix. Chop after the n, then rebuild unhappy with both pieces.

beats.onMisconception: Ask whether un- means not, or reverses an action.
beats.onRepeatedMisconception: Unlock reverses locking; the other lesson words mean not.
beats.onHelpRequest: Read the two meaning labels, then choose the job un- is doing.

beats.say: Make a word meaning not tidy.
beats.onPartial: re- makes retidy, meaning tidy again.
beats.onMisconception: We need the prefix that means not.

beats.say: Look at how the parts fit. Cover each word when you are ready.
You discovered two jobs for un-. It can mean not, or reverse an action.
